D1 Optics L5 Evaluate Reference

Scattering Phase Function: Rayleigh to Mie

grow the particle and watch symmetric dipole scattering sharpen into a forward beam

The phase function tells you where scattered light goes. Rayleigh particles give symmetric fore-and-aft lobes; large particles throw most light forward, raising the asymmetry g = ⟨cosθ⟩.

Grow the particle radius. The slate lobes are the Rayleigh dipole (1+cos²θ); the crimson curve is the size-dependent forward-peaked pattern. Light arrives from the left.
